data.title: Adobe Crushed FY25 — So Why Are Analysts Slashing ADBE Stock Targets?
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) delivered a blowout set of FY25 results, beating Wall Street expectations across the board. Revenue surged on the back of strong Creative Cloud demand and accelerating adoption of its Firefly AI tools. Yet despite the impressive performance, some of the top analysts on Wall Street are cutting their price targets on ADBE stock.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

So what's driving the caution? From AI monetization concerns to slowing enterprise growth signals, here's why analysts are turning more conservative—even as Adobe posts one of its strongest quarters to date. Following the results, ADBE stock is up over 2% as of this writing on Thursday.

Oppenheimer Cuts ADBE Stock Target

Following the results, four-star-rated analyst Brian Schwartz at Oppenheimer reduced ADBE's stock price target from $460 to $430. Schwartz noted that Adobe delivered a strong Q4 with both results and its fiscal 2026 revenue outlook topping expectations. He highlighted the company's rapid growth in AI-driven annual recurring revenue (ARR), which has now surpassed $8 billion, alongside Adobe's robust 89.3% gross margins—underscoring its strength as a leading software player.

However, Schwartz cautioned that Adobe's fiscal 2026 guidance implies high single-digit revenue growth and softer operating margins, which could create "tough optics" for investors. He lowered his price target, citing sector-wide multiple compression, and added that the latest results are unlikely to shift sentiment. He added that bulls may view the stock as undervalued, while bears remain focused on competitive pressures as the AI transition accelerates.

Other Analysts Weigh In on ADBE Stock

Likewise, Kirk Materne at Evercore ISI trimmed his price target from $450 to $425 while keeping his Buy rating. He noted that Adobe's outlook points to slower growth compared to FY25, which keeps the debate alive around the durability of demand and the company's AI-driven growth story. Materne also flagged margin concerns, with guidance calling for roughly 120 basis points of year-over-year compression.

Meanwhile, Jefferies' top-rated analyst Brent Thill cut his price target on ADBE to $500 from $590 while maintaining a Buy rating. He noted that Adobe's FY26 revenue guidance was in line with expectations, but its operating margin forecast of around 45% fell short as the company ramps up AI investments.

Thill added that while generative AI adoption is growing, any major acceleration is likely beyond FY26. Despite near-term margin pressure, he believes Adobe's risk-reward remains attractive for long-term investors.

Is ADBE a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks consensus, ADBE stock has a Moderate Buy rating, based on 17 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the last three months. The average Adobe share price target is $459.24, which implies an upside of over 32% from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Oracle Stock (ORCL) Plunges 15% after Q2, But Here's What Crowd Wisdom Shows
data.description:
Shares in cloud company and software giant Oracle (ORCL) plunged over 15% at the start of trading on Thursday following its Q2 2026 earnings results that missed Wall Street's revenue estimate and raised concerns about its AI spending.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Despite the sell-off, TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom shows retail investors have been adding Oracle to their portfolios more aggressively than major cloud peers over the past week and month.

Market Reacts to Oracle's Earnings Release

Oracle's performance has had a wide range of reactions, including dragging down stocks of artificial intelligence chip makers such as Nvidia ($Nvidia), Broadcom (AVGO), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). Widely followed cloud capacity providers and data center operators, such as CoreWeave (CRWV), have also not been spared from the downward market pressure.

Reacting to the results, Wall Street analysts have also rushed to trim their price targets on ORCL, citing the impact of its rising capital expenditure on short-term profitability. In addition, U.S. stock futures saw uneven performance on Thursday morning as the Federal Reserve's 25-basis-point rate cut was offset by Oracle's latest quarterly results.

What Does TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom Show?

On Thursday, Oracle also emerged as one of the top trends on social media platforms X as regular investors and traders weighed the performance. Some expressed disappointment, while others reaffirmed their commitment to the stock for the long-run.

Amid this volatility, TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom tool indicates that although Oracle still represents a smaller share of investors' portfolios than cloud rivals Alphabet (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Amazon (AMZN), it has seen more portfolio additions than these peers over the past week and the last 30 days. data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Dow Jones Stalls as Jobless Claims Surge by Most in Five Years
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading slightly higher on Thursday after applications for initial U.S. unemployment benefits increased by the largest amount since March 2020.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The Department of Labor announced that initial jobless claims for the week ended December 11 grew by 44,000 to 236,000 and were above the consensus estimate of 220,000. Continuing jobless claims, which track ongoing unemployment benefit recipients, were 1.838 million for the week ended November 22, below the estimate of 1.95 million.

Claims Rise but Remain Range-Bound

It's worth noting that claims data can be volatile during periods that cover holidays, like Thanksgiving. During the week ended November 29, initial jobless claims fell to a three-year low.

"As expected, last week's drop in initial jobless claims proved to be a headfake, impacted by the Thanksgiving holiday," said Allianz chief economic advisor Mohamed El-Erian in an X post.

While initial jobless claims increased, the overall figure has remained in a range between 210,000 and 250,000 for most of the year.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Oracle's (ORCL) AI Story Hits a Speed Bump — What's Next for the Stock?
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) fell after its latest quarterly update raised new doubts about the pace of its AI and cloud growth. Revenue came in at $16.1 billion, slightly below Wall Street expectations, and the company signaled that heavy spending on data-center expansion will continue, which will put pressure on margins. Earnings per share was $2.26, ahead of analysts' consensus estimate of $1.64, but the beat was not enough to ease concerns around slowing growth and rising costs.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Q2 Earnings Results Raise New Questions

Oracle has positioned itself as a major cloud provider for AI customers, but the results showed that this demand is not yet converting into revenue as quickly as investors hoped. Management pointed to slower deal timing and ongoing investment needs across its cloud and AI infrastructure, which weighed on sentiment.

Rising costs were another concern. Oracle said it must keep investing heavily to expand capacity for large AI workloads. While this supports long-term positioning, it limits near-term profit growth — a key reason the stock came under pressure.

AI Narrative Faces a Reality Check

Oracle's AI strategy has been central to its recent valuation momentum. Many investors viewed the company as a late but important entrant in the AI buildout, supported by partnerships with major enterprise clients and large GPU suppliers.

However, the latest results suggest the revenue ramp will take longer than expected. Analysts noted that backlog growth is strong, but not yet translating into faster top-line momentum. Several pointed out that Oracle needs to show clearer conversion of its AI commitments into recurring cloud revenue before confidence returns.

What to Watch Next

Analysts still see long-term value in Oracle's cloud transition, but they expect more volatility until the company delivers stronger cloud revenue, better backlog conversion, and clearer returns on its growing infrastructure spend.

For now, the quarter has paused Oracle's momentum and raised the bar for what investors want to see next.

Is ORCL Stock a Good Buy? 

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Oracle stock based on 23 Buys, 11 Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average ORCL stock price target of $346.11 indicates 55% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Oracle's Results Revive AI Bubble Concerns
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Thursday morning as the Federal Reserve's 25-basis-point rate cut was offset by software giant and cloud company Oracle's (ORCL) Q2FY26 revenue miss and elevated spending, reviving investor concerns about an AI bubble. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.44% and 0.27%, respectively, while futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.05% at 8:00 a.m. EST on December 11.

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

During Wednesday's regular trading session, all three major indexes closed higher following the Fed's rate cut decision. The Dow Jones, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq Composite rose by 1.05%, 0.67%, and 0.33%, respectively.

Investors will now focus on today's weekly initial jobless claims report and U.S. international trade data. Meanwhile, on the earnings front, companies including Broadcom (AVGO), Lululemon (LULU), and Costco (COST) are scheduled to report results.

Turning to trending stocks, Oracle stock was down more than 12% in pre-market trading following its dismal Q2 FY26 report. Also, shares of AI stocks, including CoreWeave (CRWV), Nvidia (NVDA), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), were down in reaction to Oracle's results.

In contrast, shares of Gemini Space Station (GEMI) rallied 14% after the crypto exchange secured a license to offer prediction markets to its U.S. clients.

data.title: Tesla Stock Faces Pressure as Its $1.4 Trillion Valuation Hinges on Removing Front-Seat Safety Monitors
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock slipped 0.6% in pre-market trading on Thursday, as investors remained locked in a debate over the true progress of the company's Artificial Intelligence (AI) initiatives. Few on Wall Street deny that Tesla's massive $1.4 trillion valuation is largely attributed to its aggressive AI push.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Interestingly, Deutsche Bank (DB) analyst Edison Yu estimates that Tesla's robo-taxis and humanoid robots alone account for approximately $850 billion of that valuation, with robo-taxis making up the larger portion. Tesla officially launched its robo-taxi service in Austin, Texas, in June. However, a major catch remains: Tesla still requires safety monitors in the front passenger seat.

Analysts Are Sharply Divided on Tesla's FSD Future

Analysts are sharply divided on how close Tesla is to removing those mandatory safety monitors—a key milestone investors are watching for.

Piper Sandler analyst Alexander Potter believes Tesla is nearing success. Potter recently wrote that the latest version of Full-Self Driving (FSD), Version 14, is showing significantly fewer "critical disengagements," which are instances when a human driver must take over for the car. Data from the crowdsourced teslafsdtracker.com site for FSD Version 14.1.7 shows an impressive 9,487 miles driven between human interventions, which is a huge leap from the hundreds measured in prior versions.

However, GLJ Research's Gordon Johnson strongly disagrees, calling the crowdsourced data an inadequate sign of improvement. Johnson argues that even if the tracker data were accurate, rival Alphabet's Waymo, which already operates without safety drivers, is significantly more effective, clocking more than 17,000 miles between a critical disengagement. Johnson stated he is waiting for Tesla to file formal paperwork with state regulators as a sign of true confidence in removing safety monitors and expanding the service.

Key Takeaway

It is not surprising that the two analysts maintain opposing views: Johnson rates Tesla stock at Sell, while Potter rates shares at Buy. Both, however, likely agree that the success or failure of Tesla's robo-taxi business will be the primary driver for the stock's performance in 2026.

The final takeaway from this story is simply that Tesla's $1.4 trillion valuation is tied directly to its success in deploying robo-taxis without safety monitors, but Wall Street is divided: bullish analysts cite drastically fewer human disengagements, while bears point to Waymo's superior disengagement mileage as the true benchmark.

The primary driver for Tesla's stock in 2026 will be whether CEO Elon Musk can deliver on his promise to remove those safety monitors, proving that the company's AI can close the safety gap with rivals.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analyst sentiment for Tesla (TSLA) is rated a Hold. This is based on ratings from 34 Wall Street analysts in the last three months, with 12 analysts recommending a Buy, 12 recommending a Hold, and 10 recommending a Sell. The average 12-month consensus price target for TSLA is $383.54. This average target represents a 15.04% downside from the last closing price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Is the Gold Price Surge Nothing But Hype? Americans are Not Flocking to the Precious Metal, Finds Analysis.
data.description:
The gold price lost some shine today following the decision yesterday by the U.S. Federal Reserve to cut interest rates. Banking analysis which showed that Americans have not found the precious metal as much of a safe haven as previously thought also hit its allure.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Uncertainty Over Rates

The Fed cut rates by 25 basis points in a split vote, putting it in a range of 3.50% to 3.75% – its lowest level in three years.

Despite lower interest rates being good for the precious metal, the spot gold price was however flat at $4,207. That is likely down to caution over the future policy direction of the Federal Reserve. It gave guidance that borrowing costs may not fall again soon as policymakers wait for clearer evidence of a cooling labour market and inflation that, in their words, "remains somewhat elevated."

"The Fed's accompanying statement, which removed the forward guidance on additional cuts, alongside two hawkish dissents, gave this cut a hawkish flavour," said Max Stainton, senior global macro strategist at Fidelity International. "However, the reintroduction of quantitative easing (QE) targeting $40bn bill purchases a month, alongside a set of dots which retained a cut next year and the year after, suggests there is still a large bulk of the FOMC who see interest rates as being able to fall further before hitting a neutral resting point."

Lukewarm American Interest

The gold price has been on a record-breaking run this year – see below – as economic and geopolitical uncertainty drove investors to find a safe haven for their cash.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Oracle (ORCL) Stock Tumbles 13% — And Billionaire David Tepper May Have Seen It Coming. Morgan Stanley Sounds Caution.
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) investors woke up to a rough day, with the stock tumbling about 13% as the company's latest quarterly report landed with a thud. On the surface, Oracle delivered double-digit revenue growth and another solid jump in cloud sales, but the details told a far less comfortable story.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

A mix of underwhelming execution and rising costs set the tone for the sell-off. Oracle has been positioning itself as a major AI-infrastructure player, pouring billions into datacenter expansion to meet growing GPU-as-a-Service demand. But the market wanted reassurance that these investments are paying off quickly and profitably. Instead, revenue came in lighter than anticipated, cloud growth failed to hit the top of the guided range, and margins took a hit. The company is spending aggressively, yet not producing the kind of operating leverage investors expected at this stage of the AI cycle.

Interestingly, one billionaire may have read the situation early. David Tepper, the influential investor behind Appaloosa Management, had been easing out of Oracle for more than a year, starting from a position of about 2.3 million shares in Q1 2024. He steadily trimmed that stake quarter after quarter and ultimately sold his remaining 150,000 shares in Q3 2025. Nothing in Tepper's commentary suggested he predicted a collapse, but in the light of today's drop, his steady retreat now looks, at the very least, well-timed.

The concerns that weighed on Tepper's conviction echo loudly through Wall Street's reaction today. Morgan Stanley's Keith Weiss didn't mince words about the mood around Oracle. In his view, "Cloud growth at the low-end of the guide with building pressure on gross margins and op margins may further sap investor confidence in ORCL's ability to execute efficiently" as it tries to scale its GPU-as-a-Service business. The analyst added that, as things stand, the shares are "lacking a clear catalyst."

Weiss also suggested that the market's harsh reaction says something deeper about sentiment. The analyst believes that investors "may be increasingly losing confidence in Oracle's ability to convert this large (and still expanding) backlog into durable, profitable, revenue streams." In his view, the quarter raised fresh doubts about the quality and reliability of the growth Oracle is promising.

Looking ahead, Weiss expressed concern that some of Oracle's recent financing and chip-sourcing strategies "sap confidence that Oracle can achieve compelling economics" on its AI-infrastructure ambitions. And he warned that before investors can fully buy into Oracle's long-term targets, "greater confidence" is needed that the GPUaaS business will actually lift earnings and free cash flow – not weigh them down.

With that rising uncertainty, Weiss put his price target and estimates "under review," while assigning ORCL an Equal-weight (i.e., Neutral) rating. (To watch Weiss' track record, click here)

As for the broader analyst community? The Street remains cautiously optimistic. ORCL carries a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 35 reviews, broken down into 23 Buys, 11 Holds, and 1 Sell. The average price target of $346.11 suggests potential upside of 55% from yesterday's close. (See ORCL stock analysis)
data.author: Ben Yoffe

data.title: Here's Why AXIL Brands Stock (AXIL) Soared Today
data.description:
AXIL Brands (AXIL) stock surged on Thursday after the hearing performance and protection technology company announced a national retail distribution agreement with Walmart (WMT). With this agreement, more than 3,700 Walmart stores across the U.S. will start carrying its AXIL X30 LT in-ear hearing protection plugs. This launch will happen in February 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

AXIL Brands noted that its distribution agreement with Walmart will represent the company's largest single retail placement to date. The company said that this agreement, combined with other expansion efforts, positions it for accelerated growth and higher brand visibility in 2026 and the years to follow.

Jeff Toghraie, Chairman and CEO at AXIL Brands, said, "We are thrilled that Walmart has chosen the X30 LT for nationwide rollout. This partnership is a testament to the performance, value, and consumer demand for our products and will make our best-in-class hearing protection and enhancement technology available to millions of consumers who shop at Walmart every day."

AXIL Brands Stock Movement Today

AXIL Brands stock was up 75.7% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 1.57% fall yesterday. The stock has rallied 30.05% year-to-date and 29.05% year-to-date.

With today's Walmart news came heavy trading of AXIL stock, as some 12 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is about 22,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Why AI Chip Stocks NVDA, AVGO, and AMD Are Sliding in Pre-Market
data.description:
AI chip stocks took a hit in pre-hours trading on Thursday after Oracle (ORCL) delivered a disappointing Q2 earnings report. Shares of Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell 1.5%, while Broadcom (AVGO) dropped 1.2% after Oracle's weak revenue report and higher expense guidance raised concerns about slowing enterprise AI spending. Meanwhile, Oracle (ORCL) plunged over 13% in pre-hours trading.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

For Q2 FY26, Oracle reported earnings per share of $2.26, easily beating the $1.64 analysts expected. However, revenues of $16.1 billion fell just short of the $16.2 billion Wall Street was looking for. Additionally, Oracle's forecast for third-quarter revenue growth came in at 16% to 18%, falling short of analysts' expectations for 19.4% growth.

What Went Wrong with Oracle's Results?

More than the Q2 numbers, Oracle's weaker outlook and sharply higher spending plans raised new worries about the AI bubble. Oracle's rising expense outlook intensified concerns about its growing debt load to support its AI infrastructure push.

This uncertainty spilled over into the broader AI sector, pressuring stocks like Nvidia, Broadcom, and AMD as investors reassessed near-term demand for AI infrastructure. Analysts are also questioning how quickly these companies can turn their heavy investments into meaningful revenue, especially as competition in the AI market continues to heat up.

What Lies Ahead for Investors?

With AI demand expected to drive the next major phase of tech growth, Oracle's cautious tone raised concerns for investors.

Looking ahead, investors should brace for continued volatility in AI-related stocks as the market reassesses demand following Oracle's weak outlook. While long-term AI fundamentals remain strong, near-term sentiment may stay shaky until companies show clearer profit visibility from their AI investments.
